claim: Underspecified problems cause agents to take shortcuts and implement features the wrong way; planning ensures full communication of requirements upfront
actionable: Before delegating a complex task to a coding agent, write a detailed specification or use plan mode to iteratively clarify requirements with the agent.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: Sub-agents enable running multiple specialized agents in parallel on different aspects of the same task, combining results to get multiple perspectives
actionable: Use sub-agent features in your coding agent tool (available in Copilot, Claude Code, etc.) to parallelize code review or analysis across different models (e.g., Haiku, Gemini, Opus) to catch issues each model specializes in.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: With coding agents, code production becomes so cheap that prototypes are essentially free and code no longer needs to be treated as a precious asset to maintain
actionable: Shift your mindset: don't hesitate to generate throwaway code, experiment with multiple implementations, or regenerate code when requirements change—the generation cost is now negligible compared to design and validation.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: Skills are reusable, shareable markdown-based documents that encapsulate agent capabilities and are progressively disclosed to avoid context window bloat
actionable: Create skills by defining markdown specs with name, description, and detailed workflow; store in source control and share with your team so agents automatically discover and use them as needed.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: Agents must have access to actual feedback loops (compilation, linting, tests, running the real application) to detect and fix their own errors instead of requiring manual correction cycles
actionable: Configure your agent with the ability to compile code, run tests, and start your application; give it browser automation skills so it can interact with the UI to verify features work end-to-end.
confidence_notes: Core principle demonstrated repeatedly; breaking this pattern leads to frustrating correction loops.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: Plan mode in coding agents structures complex work into phases with validation checkpoints at each stage, allowing humans to verify observable UI changes rather than just trusting code output
actionable: Use plan mode (shift+tab in Copilot CLI) to work with the agent on a comprehensive implementation plan; add validation steps at each phase describing what the user should see in the UI.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: Different AI models (Haiku, Gemini, Opus) have distinct strengths: Haiku responds fast but with shallow insights, Gemini is more anxious and catches architectural concerns, Opus is most rigorous but slower
actionable: When reviewing code or analyzing complex features, run the task across multiple models in parallel sub-agents to leverage their different risk profiles: Haiku for speed, Gemini for breadth, Opus for depth.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: High-performing teams using coding agents can ship 10+ non-trivial, fully-tested features per developer per week (Sanderson's team: 100 PRs/week, ~50% trivial, ~50% serious multi-day features)
actionable: Set aggressive productivity targets and measure merge rate; expect 10+ feature-complete PRs per developer per week when using agents effectively, but prepare for code review bottlenecks.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: Code review throughput is now a critical bottleneck: teams can generate PRs faster than humans can review them, which is an unsolved infrastructure problem
actionable: Invest in automated code review tools (using agents to review agent-generated code) or reduce code review requirements; do not expect manual review to keep pace with agent productivity.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: CLI-based coding agents (like Copilot CLI, Claude Code, Gemini) feel 'addictive' because they provide visible progress within 5-10 seconds, unlike IDE-integrated tools
actionable: Use CLI-based agents for high-velocity prototyping and feature work where rapid feedback cycles matter more than IDE integration; they allow fast iteration on code outside specific projects.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: Agents can successfully fix bugs in languages the human doesn't speak (e.g., Rust) by investigating repositories, finding the issue, submitting a PR—empowering non-specialists to contribute cross-language fixes
actionable: Don't skip contributing to projects in unfamiliar languages; use a coding agent to clone repos, investigate issues, generate fixes, and submit PRs autonomously.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: Coding agent SDKs (Node, Python, .NET, Go) allow embedding agent capabilities into applications via APIs, enabling non-software-engineering tasks (e.g., slide deck generation, issue estimation)
actionable: Use the Copilot SDK or equivalent to integrate agentic reasoning into your application; define custom tools via strongly-typed method definitions and let the agent choose when to invoke them.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: For complex features, iterative prototyping via agents (trying multiple architectural approaches in parallel) is faster than upfront design meetings because you learn by doing and compare outcomes
actionable: Skip heavy upfront design for prototype-stage work; instead, have the agent generate 2-3 different implementations in parallel, compare results, and choose the best approach based on actual outcomes.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: Large output handling in agents saves context by storing large files on disk and having sub-agents read them on-demand, preventing context window overload during multi-file reviews
actionable: For large PRs or documents, enable large output handling so the agent stores content to disk and fetches it incrementally, preventing context exhaustion.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026

claim: Rapid code generation is changing how developers interact with their tools: relying on voice/natural language commands feels more natural than manual typing after sustained agent use
actionable: Be aware that extended use of coding agents may change your muscle memory and typing habits; maintain manual coding practice if you want to preserve low-level coding skills.
source_title: Keynote: AI-Powered App Development - Steve Sanderson - NDC London 2026
